title: Aureline locality/tenancy/key-mode seed row
description: Boundary schema for one locality_tenancy_keymode_row_record published by the M1 locality/tenancy/key-mode vocabulary seed. Each row binds one protected surface family to (1) a locality_class drawn from the closed local_only/remote_target/provider_linked/managed_control_plane_bearing/mirrored/unknown_locality vocabulary, (2) a tenancy_scope_class drawn from the closed tenancy vocabulary that distinguishes not-applicable-local, single-user-local, single-user-managed-tenant, org-tenant, multi-tenant-isolated, multi-tenant-shared, and an explicit unknown-tenancy uncertainty token, (3) a key_storage_mode_class drawn from the closed key-mode vocabulary that distinguishes not-applicable-local, local-storage-only, os-keychain-backed, byok-user-managed, provider-managed-key, managed-service-kms, and an explicit unknown-key-mode uncertainty token, (4) a local_safe_fallback_class so a managed or provider-linked row cannot silently delete its local-safe fallback by omission, (5) a data_residency_disclosure_class drawn from a closed residency-disclosure vocabulary that names what the surface tells the user about residency without claiming product depth M1 does not have, and (6) a named failure drill the validation lane reproduces under --force-drill. This schema is a vocabulary contract, not a managed-tenancy or key-management runtime: it does not implement residency enforcement, tenant isolation, key custody, or rotation.

locality_tenancy_keymode_row_schema_vers.description: Integer schema version for locality/tenancy/key-mode rows. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

locality_tenancy_keymode_profile_id.description: Stable, machine-readable id for the locality/tenancy/key-mode profile row (for example local_only.editor_buffer_save). Lowercase dot/underscore/hyphen segments only so the id is safe to log, safe in support exports, and safe in release artifacts.

surface_family_class.description: Coarse surface family the row characterises. M1 distinguishes the protected surfaces named in the boundary manifest plus the prototype managed/control-plane-bearing classes the seed must label without inventing product depth.

locality_class.description: Locality class. 'local_only' means the surface works with no network, no sign-in, and no hosted service. 'remote_target' means the surface attaches to a user-controlled remote target (SSH/container/WSL/dev VM) the user named themselves; the surface narrows to local-core editing when the target is absent. 'provider_linked' means an optional user-supplied provider (BYOK / customer IdP / user-owned LSP / local model) attaches to the local surface; without a provider the surface narrows but is not removed. 'managed_control_plane_bearing' means the surface carries a hosted control-plane bearer claim (managed sync, managed companion, hosted optional surface). M1 seeds the vocabulary only; no managed control-plane is enforced. 'mirrored' means reachable via a signed offline mirror/bundle in constrained-connectivity profiles. 'unknown_locality' is the explicit uncertainty token; surfaces that cannot truthfully name their locality MUST publish this rather than guess.

tenancy_scope_class.description: Tenancy scope. 'not_applicable_local_only' means the surface has no tenancy concept (local-only). 'single_user_local' means a single-user surface that does not cross a tenancy boundary (a user-attached SSH target, a local-only AI model). 'single_user_managed_tenant' means a managed surface scoped to a single user under a managed tenant. 'org_tenant' means scoped to one organisation tenant. 'multi_tenant_isolated' means the surface is one tenant in a multi-tenant managed service with explicit isolation guarantees the seed only labels, never implements. 'multi_tenant_shared' means the surface shares state with other tenants intentionally. 'unknown_tenancy' is the explicit uncertainty token; surfaces that cannot truthfully name their tenancy MUST publish this rather than guess.

key_storage_mode_class.description: Key/storage mode. 'not_applicable_local_only' means the surface neither holds keys nor stores user secrets. 'local_storage_only' means key material/data lives in the local profile only. 'os_keychain_backed' means the local OS keychain or equivalent platform key store. 'byok_user_managed' means the user supplies their own key material the surface treats as opaque. 'provider_managed_key' means the user-attached provider manages the key. 'managed_service_kms' means a managed KMS is in scope (label only at M1). 'unknown_key_mode' is the explicit uncertainty token; surfaces that cannot truthfully name their key mode MUST publish this rather than guess.

local_safe_fallback_class.description: Local-safe fallback posture the row declares. 'local_safe_fallback_present' means the surface continues to function offline on the same hardware with no narrowing of its M1 baseline. 'local_safe_fallback_narrowed' means a strict local subset of the surface continues to function with documented narrowing (cross-repo nav unavailable, ai narrows to local-only model, etc); absence narrows the claim instead of removing the surface. 'local_safe_fallback_unavailable' means the surface depends on the named remote/managed link to operate at all; M1 surfaces that publish this MUST be optional, MUST disclose it in Help/About/service-health, and MUST NOT be on the local-only floor.

data_residency_disclosure_class.description: What the surface tells the user about data residency in M1. 'residency_local_device_only' means the row never moves user data off the local device. 'residency_user_owned_remote_target' means user data may rest on a remote target the user named themselves. 'residency_provider_default' means the user-attached provider's default applies; the surface discloses the provider's posture but does not enforce a region. 'residency_managed_tenant_documented_region' means the managed surface documents its region but the seed does NOT enforce residency in M1. 'residency_unknown' is the explicit uncertainty token; surfaces that cannot truthfully disclose residency MUST publish this rather than imply a region.

truth_badge_class.description: Closed badge vocabulary the seed maps onto. M1 surfaces may render these via the Help/About truth prototype and the service-health prototype. 'managed_disclosure_badge' is the prototype label managed/control-plane-bearing rows use to keep their disclosure honest.

diagnostic_surface_class.description: Closed diagnostic-surface vocabulary that names where the row's locality/tenancy/key-mode tokens are intended to be visible in M1.

drill_id.description: Stable id for the row's named failure drill, drawn from the envelope's failure_drill_id_vocabulary.

forced_input.description: Forced row mutation applied under --force-drill so the runner can reproduce the named expected_check_id from a clean tree.

expected_check_id.description: Exact check_id the runner must report under --force-drill.

actionable_next_action.description: Short reviewer-readable next action when the drill fires unexpectedly.

failure_drill.description: Named failure drill the validation lane reproduces under --force-drill. Required on every row so omissions stay loud.

truth_badge_classes.description: Closed badge classes the row claims to render. Empty list is forbidden so a row cannot quietly opt out of public-truth surfaces; rows that do not render must publish managed_disclosure_badge or one of the explicit badge classes.

diagnostic_surface_classes.description: Closed diagnostic surfaces that consume the row's tokens. Empty list is forbidden so a row cannot publish vocabulary that no surface reads.

local_core_continuity.description: Required reviewer-facing description of what continues to work with no network, no sign-in, and no managed/control-plane reachability. Cannot be empty; managed and provider-linked rows MUST document the local-safe fallback verbatim so the seed cannot quietly drop local-core continuity.

absence_narrows_to.description: Required reviewer-facing description of what the row narrows to when its remote/managed/provider dependency is absent. Empty is forbidden; absence narrows a claim rather than removing the surface.

upstream_boundary_manifest_row_refs.description: Optional refs into the upstream internal boundary manifest matrix's row_ids the row projects from. The validation lane resolves these against artifacts/governance/m1_open_local_capability_matrix.yaml so the seed cannot quietly drift from its upstream.

upstream_provider_registry_refs.description: Optional refs into the upstream connected-provider/provider-descriptor seed when the row applies to provider-linked or managed surfaces. The validation lane resolves the consumer ref on disk but does not crawl the IDs (the upstream may not enumerate ids in M1).

owner_dri.description: Named owner DRI for the locality/tenancy/key-mode profile row.

notes.description: Optional short reviewer-facing note about the row.

allOf.description: local_only rows MUST publish tenancy_scope_class in {not_applicable_local_only, single_user_local}, key_storage_mode_class in {not_applicable_local_only, local_storage_only, os_keychain_backed}, data_residency_disclosure_class = residency_local_device_only, and local_safe_fallback_class = local_safe_fallback_present.

managed_control_plane_bearing rows MUST publish tenancy_scope_class in {single_user_managed_tenant, org_tenant, multi_tenant_isolated, multi_tenant_shared, unknown_tenancy}, key_storage_mode_class in {byok_user_managed, provider_managed_key, managed_service_kms, unknown_key_mode}, data_residency_disclosure_class in {residency_managed_tenant_documented_region, residency_provider_default, residency_unknown}, and disclose a local_safe_fallback_class explicitly (cannot be omitted; a managed row MUST NOT publish residency_local_device_only).
unknown_locality rows MUST publish unknown_tenancy and unknown_key_mode; rows that cannot name their locality MUST NOT silently certify their tenancy or key mode.

provider_linked rows MUST NOT publish tenancy_scope_class = not_applicable_local_only or key_storage_mode_class = not_applicable_local_only; provider-linked surfaces must name their tenancy and key-mode classes (including the unknown_* uncertainty tokens) rather than silently fall back to the local-only sentinels.
